page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB memory-per-core ratio. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier across most standard workloads. The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. In synthetic testing, the instance achieved a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 15477.28 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 9153.0. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 847.6 GB/sec, dropping to 93.71 GB/sec for large uncached blocks.

For application-level workloads, the server demonstrates solid throughput, processing 15.34 million Redis SET operations per second and delivering 18.93 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. However, resource constraints are evident in memory-intensive or AI tasks; while it handles small 135M LLM text generation at 233.74 tokens/sec, prompt processing for a medium 7B LLM drops to a weak 31.09 tokens/sec. The instance represents a balanced, cost-efficient option for compute-bound tasks that do not require high memory-to-CPU ratios or local SSD storage. Appropriate workloads include web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor at 2.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under a KVM hypervisor, it provides 32.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but offers a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate average performance, with a multi-core Geekbench score of 9153.0 and a static web serving throughput of 18.93 GB/sec. While it supports AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration, its memory-to-CPU ratio limits its efficiency in large-scale AI tasks, as seen in its lower 7B LLM prompt processing speed. It is best suited for compute-intensive workloads, web serving, and software compilation.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32.0 GB RAM, and 10 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for web serving and compilation workloads.

tagline: A dedicated x86_64 compute-optimized platform featuring sixteen Intel Xeon cores for balanced web serving and processing workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Supports AVX-512, vector extensions, and cryptographic acceleration
Suited for web serving, software compilation, and compute-bound tasks
